snp4.9:1.1:
[Māgandiya, a brahman, offers his daughter to the Buddha,
who replies:] “On seeing [the daughters of Māra] —Discontent, Craving, & Passion—
snp4.9:1.2: there wasn’t even the desire for sex.
snp4.9:1.3: So what would I want with this, filled with urine & excrement?
snp4.9:1.4:
I wouldn’t want to touch it even with my foot.”
Māgandiya:
snp4.9:2.1: “If you don’t want this gem of a woman, coveted
snp4.9:2.3: then for what sort of viewpoint, habit, practice, life,
snp4.9:2.4: attainment of [further] becoming do you argue?”
snp4.9:3.1:
The Buddha:
“‘I argue for this’ doesn’t occur to one
snp4.9:3.3: when considering what’s grasped among doctrines.
snp4.9:3.4: Looking for what is ungrasped with regard to views,
snp4.9:3.5: and detecting inner peace, I saw.”
snp4.9:4.1:
Māgandiya:
“Sage, you speak of not grasping at any theorized judgments.
snp4.9:4.5:
What does it mean?
How is it, by the enlightened, proclaimed?”
snp4.9:5.1:
The Buddha:
“He doesn’t speak of purity in connection with view, learning, knowledge,
snp4.9:5.4: Nor is it found by a person through lack of view, of learning, of knowledge,
snp4.9:5.7: at peace, independent, one wouldn’t long for becoming.”
snp4.9:6.1:
Māgandiya:
“Well, if he doesn’t speak of purity in connection with view, learning, knowledge,
snp4.9:6.4: and it isn’t found by a person through lack of view, of learning, of knowledge,
snp4.9:6.6: it seems to me that this teaching’s simply confused,
snp4.9:6.7: for some assume a purity in terms of —by means of— a view.”
snp4.9:7.1:
The Buddha:
“Asking questions dependent on view,
snp4.9:7.3: you’re confused by the things you have grasped.
snp4.9:7.4: And so you don’t glimpse even the slightest notion [of what I am saying].
snp4.9:7.5: That’s why you think it’s confused.
snp4.9:8.3: whereas to one unaffected by these three,
snp4.9:9.1: Of what would the brahman say ‘true’
snp4.9:9.2: or ‘false,’ with whom would he dispute?
snp4.9:9.3: With whom would he join in dispute, he in whom ‘equal,’ ‘unequal’ are not?
snp4.9:10.1: Having abandoned home, living free from society,
snp4.9:10.2: the sage in villages creates no intimacies.
snp4.9:10.4: he wouldn’t engage with people in quarrelsome debate.
snp4.9:11.1: Those things aloof from which he should go about in the world:
snp4.9:11.2: The Nāga wouldn’t take them up & argue for them.
snp4.9:11.5: so the sage, an exponent of peace, without greed,
snp4.9:11.6: is unsmeared by sensuality & the world.
snp4.9:12.1: An attainer-of-knowledge isn’t measured made proud by views or what’s thought,
snp4.9:12.2: for he isn’t fashioned of them.
snp4.9:12.3: He wouldn’t be led by action, learning;
snp4.9:12.4: doesn’t reach a conclusion in any entrenchments.
snp4.9:13.1: For one dispassionate toward perception there are no snares;
snp4.9:13.2: for one released by discernment, no delusions.
snp4.9:13.3: Those who grasp at perceptions & views
snp4.9:13.4: go about clashing in the world.”
